steps.title: Identify the slope-intercept form
steps.content:
A linear function has the form $y = mx + b$ where $m$ is the slope and $b$ is the y-intercept.

From the problem:
  • Slope: $m = \frac{1}{3}$
  • The graph passes through $(0, 2)$, which is the y-intercept, so $b = 2$

steps.title: Substitute to find f(x)
steps.content:
Substitute $m = \frac{1}{3}$ and $b = 2$ into $y = mx + b$:

$$f(x) = \frac{1}{3}x + 2$$

That matches choice A — A is correct.
